_README: LLM-derived 0–3 axis scores for scotches (axes: smoke, sweet, oak, vanilla, fruit, body). Smoke = peat character (defining axis for scotch — replaces bourbon/rye's spice). Three archetype placeholders (Islay Scotch, Peated/Unpeated Single Malt) scored from their generic-style descriptions; specific bottles scored from their own tasting notes.

215.name: Ardbeg Heavy Vapours Scotch
215.reasoning: 2023 Feis Ile release, 46% ABV. First Ardbeg distilled without the purifier = heavier oils and phenols. Tasting: intense peat, charcoal, licorice. Ardbeg is already the smoky end of Islay; no-purifier intensifies it. Heavy oily body.

637.name: Compass Box Brûlée Royale Blended Malt Scotch Whisky
637.reasoning: Limited-edition crème brûlée-themed blended malt. Primarily Clynelish (Highlands) + Benrinnes (Speyside) + Speyburn, with ~0.5% Ardbeg as a 'kiss' of smoke evoking torched sugar. 49% ABV. Sweet/vanilla heavy by design.

373.name: Islay Scotch
373.reasoning: Archetype placeholder — 'peated single malt from Islay, Scotland.' Scored as the generic Islay style: heavy smoke, dry, modest oak/fruit. 43% ABV.

216.name: Lagavulin 11 Year Caribbean Rum Cask Finish Offerman Edition
216.reasoning: Nick Offerman Edition #4. 11 yrs bourbon/sherry casks + 8mo Caribbean rum cask finish, 46% ABV. Lagavulin's heavy peat softened by sweet molasses from rum finish.

217.name: Lagavulin Offerman Edition 11 Years (2019)
217.reasoning: First Offerman edition (2019). 11-yr Islay single malt. Peaty character with dried fruit notes. No special cask finish, so drier than the 2023 rum-finished version.

218.name: Oban 14 Single Malt Scotch Whisky
218.reasoning: 14-yr Highland Coastal single malt, 43% ABV. 'Gateway to the Isles' — coastal/briny character without heavy peat. Tasting: dried figs, honey, maritime salt. Low smoke (Highlands, not Islay), high fruit (dried figs).

472.name: Peated Single Malt Scotch
472.reasoning: Archetype placeholder — 'rule: smoke is dominant and unmistakable.' Scored as the generic peated-SM style: heavy smoke, modest everything else.

473.name: Unpeated Single Malt Scotch
473.reasoning: Archetype placeholder — 'rule: malt, fruit, and oak without dominant smoke.' Scored as the generic Speyside/Highland-ish unpeated style.
